subject: History
title: Socialism in Europe and the Russian Revolution

questions.text: Who were the 'Liberals' in 19th century Europe?
options: Those who wanted to keep the old regime
Those who wanted a nation which tolerated all religions
Those who wanted to abolish private property
questions.explanation: Liberals wanted a nation which tolerated all religions and opposed the uncontrolled power of dynastic rulers.

questions.text: Liberals opposed 'Universal Adult Franchise'. What does this mean?
options: Opposed right to vote for all citizens
questions.explanation: Liberals did not believe in universal adult franchise; they felt men of property mainly should have the vote.

questions.text: Who wanted a government based on the majority of a country's population?
questions.explanation: Radicals wanted a nation in which government was based on the majority of a country’s population.

questions.text: Which group of people generally opposed both Liberals and Radicals initially?
questions.explanation: Conservatives were generally opposed to radicals and liberals, but after the French Revolution, they accepted that some change was inevitable.

questions.text: Who sought to build a cooperative community called 'New Harmony' in Indiana (USA)?
questions.explanation: Robert Owen was a leading English manufacturer who sought to build a cooperative community.

questions.text: Karl Marx argued that the profit of capitalists was produced by:
questions.explanation: Marx argued that industrial society was 'capitalist' and workers produced the profit which was taken by owners.

questions.text: Which international body was formed to coordinate socialist efforts?
questions.explanation: By the 1870s, socialist ideas spread through Europe and an international body called the Second International was formed.

questions.text: Who ruled Russia in 1914?
questions.explanation: In 1914, Tsar Nicholas II ruled Russia and its empire.

questions.text: What was the main occupation of about 85% of the Russian population in the early 20th century?
questions.explanation: At the beginning of the twentieth century, the vast majority (about 85%) of Russia’s people were agriculturists.

questions.text: The Russian Social Democratic Workers Party (RSDWP) followed the ideas of:
questions.explanation: The RSDWP, formed in 1898, followed Marx’s ideas.

questions.text: Which socialist group believed that peasants were 'natural socialists'?
questions.explanation: The Socialist Revolutionary Party (formed in 1900) believed Russian peasants were natural socialists because of their communal land distribution.

questions.text: The Bolshevik group was led by:
questions.explanation: Vladimir Lenin led the Bolshevik group and believed the party should be disciplined and control its membership.

questions.text: What event triggered the 1905 Revolution in Russia?
questions.explanation: Bloody Sunday (Jan 1905), where over 100 workers were killed by police at the Winter Palace, started the revolution.

questions.text: What was the 'Duma'?
questions.explanation: During the 1905 Revolution, the Tsar allowed the creation of an elected consultative Parliament or Duma.

questions.text: During World War I, Russia fought against:
questions.explanation: In World War I, Russia was part of the Allies and fought against the Central Powers, including Germany and Austria.

questions.text: The 1917 February Revolution led to the abdication of:
questions.explanation: Following the mass protests in Petrograd in February 1917, the Tsar abdicated his throne.

questions.text: What were Lenin's three demands in the 'April Theses'?
options: War be ended, land to peasants, banks be nationalised
War be continued, property to rich, high taxes
Abolish Duma, exile Tsar, kill clergy
questions.explanation: Lenin's April Theses demanded ending the war, transferring land to peasants, and nationalising banks.

questions.text: Who was the Prime Minister of the Provisional Government after February 1917?
questions.explanation: Alexander Kerensky was the Prime Minister of the Provisional Government.

questions.text: On which date did the Bolshevik uprising (October Revolution) begin?
questions.explanation: The uprising began on 24 October 1917, and by nightfall, the city of Petrograd was under Bolshevik control.

questions.text: After the October Revolution, the Bolshevik Party was renamed as:
questions.explanation: The Bolshevik Party renamed itself the Russian Communist Party (Bolshevik).

questions.text: During the Civil War in Russia, the 'Reds' referred to:
questions.explanation: The 'Reds' were the Bolsheviks, while 'Greens' were Socialist Revolutionaries and 'Whites' were pro-Tsarists.

questions.text: Who introduced the 'Collectivisation' program in Russia?
questions.explanation: Stalin introduced the collectivisation program to solve the problem of grain shortages.

questions.text: What were 'Kulaks'?
questions.explanation: Kulaks were the well-to-do peasants whom Stalin targeted during collectivisation.

questions.text: The name of the Russian parliament created in 1905 was:
questions.explanation: The Duma was the parliament granted by the Tsar after the 1905 Revolution.

questions.text: Socialists were against:
questions.explanation: Socialists saw private property as the root of all social ills of the time.

questions.text: In the context of Russia, what was 'Petrograd'?
options: A new name for St. Petersburg
questions.explanation: During WWI, St. Petersburg (a German name) was renamed Petrograd.

questions.text: The process of central planning in Russia led to:
questions.explanation: Central planning involved Five-Year Plans that boosted industrial production and economic growth.

questions.text: Who was the influential monk who advised the Tsarina Alexandra?
questions.explanation: The monk Rasputin made the autocracy unpopular due to his influence over the royal family.

questions.text: Collective farms in Russia were called:
questions.explanation: Kolkhoz was the name given to collective farms during Stalin's era.

questions.text: The main cause of the 1905 revolution was:
questions.explanation: A combination of military defeat and economic hardship led to the 1905 uprising.

questions.text: When did the Bolsheviks seize the Winter Palace?
questions.explanation: The Winter Palace was shelled and seized during the October Revolution in 1917.

questions.text: Socialism became a popular idea in Europe by the:
questions.explanation: By the mid-nineteenth century in Europe, socialism was a well-known body of ideas.

questions.text: What was the 'Comintern'?
options: International union of pro-Bolshevik socialist parties
questions.explanation: The Comintern was an international organization of pro-Bolshevik socialist parties.

questions.text: Which year was the Soviet Union (USSR) formed?
questions.explanation: The USSR was created by the Bolsheviks from the Russian empire in December 1922.

questions.text: Jadidists were ______ reformers within the Russian empire.
questions.explanation: Jadidists were Muslim reformers who wanted modernised Islam to lead their societies.

questions.text: Who was the leader of the 'Whites' during the Civil War?
questions.explanation: The 'Whites' were the pro-Tsarist forces opposed to the Bolshevik revolution.

questions.text: The Labour Party in Britain was formed by ______ in 1905.
questions.explanation: Socialists and trade unionists formed a Labour Party in Britain and a Socialist Party in France.

questions.text: Tsar Nicholas II dismissed the first Duma within:
questions.explanation: The Tsar dismissed the first Duma within 75 days as he did not want any reduction in his power.

questions.text: The industrial revolution led to:
questions.explanation: Industrialization brought men, women and children to factories but with poor conditions and low wages.

questions.text: Who wrote 'The Communist Manifesto'?
questions.explanation: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels were the authors of this seminal work on socialism.
